title: Computer Science & Engineering Professionals - Mid to Expert Level (Maryland)
description: NSA is in search of experienced Computer Science and Engineering professionals to solve complex problems, test innovative approaches, and research new solutions. If you want to develop technologies and tools to help protect national security interests, join our team as part of the world's most advanced team of computer science and engineering professionals!

title: Public Notice - Engineering (Direct Hire)
description: Positions advertised are in various organizations at Kennedy Space Center. NASA may fill positions in any of the following series and at any grade level listed in this announcement (GS-0801, 0819, 0830, 0850, 0855, 0861 at the GS-11/12/13/14/15 levels).

description: Positions advertised are in various organizations at Kennedy Space Center. NASA may fill positions in any of the following series and at any grade level listed in this announcement (GS-2210 at the GS-09/11/12/13/14/15 levels).

title: Public Notice - Engineering Technician (Direct Hire)

description: Positions advertised are in various organizations at Kennedy Space Center. NASA may fill positions in any of the following series and at any grade level listed in this announcement (GS-0301, 0343, 0346 at the GS-09/11/12/13/14/15 levels).

title: Evidence Custodian
description: This position is located in the Drug Enforcement Administration (DEA), Los Angeles Field Division. This position(s) primary purpose is to receive, document, store, and dispose of non-drug evidence, drug evidence, bulk drug evidence, and/or stockpiles for a Field Office.

title: Information Technology Specialist (Direct Hire)
description: As a Systems, Applications, and Products in Data Processing (SAP) Technical Subject Matter Expert, you will be responsible for SAP technical design and configuration, ensuring compliant financial processes, audit readiness, and system integrity. You will oversee Advanced Business Application Programing (ABAP) development, manage year-end processing, and guide cross-functional teams through full lifecycle delivery as we move to the SAP S/4 High Performance Analytic Appliance (HANA) environment.

title: Operations Research Analyst
description: This position is part of the Office of Cybersecurity, Energy Security and Emergency Response, Department of Energy. As a Operations Research Analyst, you will provide expert analysis and technically authoritative guidance to originate and perform mathematical, statistical, economic, and/or other analysis projects pertaining to the energy sector, an energy sub-sector, scientific research, and/or national security, and to maintain and enhance models for use in simulation studies.

title: Information Technology Specialist
description: This position is part of the Office of Intelligence and Counterintelligence, Department of Energy. As a Information Technology Specialist, you will support the Department of Energy Counterintelligence (CI) mission, you will provide Information Technology (IT) expertise to DOE CI mission activities, including investigations, awareness, analysis, insider threat, cyber forensics, and more benefitting DOE elements at headquarters, field offices, and the national laboratories.

title: COMPUTER SCIENTIST
description: Defense Finance and Accounting Services (DFAS) mission is to lead the Department of Defense (DoD) in finance and accounting by ensuring the delivery of efficient, exceptional quality pay and financial information.

title: Senior Data Scientist
locations: District of Columbia, District of Columbia
description: As a Senior Data Scientist, you will apply expert knowledge of data science and quantitative analytical methods to organize, analyze, interpret, and derive meaningful insight from structured and unstructured data in support of real-world problem solving. Location of position: Federal Acquisition Service (FAS), Office of Acquisition Solution Development. We are currently filling one vacancy, but additional vacancies may be filled as needed.

title: Senior Software Engineer

title: Applications Software Engineer
description: As an IT Specialist (Applications Software) with the US Office of Personnel Management, Products and Data Engineering Divisions, you will serve as a senior software engineer responsible for designing, developing, integrating, modernizing, and sustaining enterprise application systems and services that support OPM mission requirements.

title: Electrical Engineer, AST, Electrical Systems (Direct Hire)
description: As an Electrical Engineer, you will perform professional engineering work involving the design, analysis, testing, and compliance assessment of Flight Termination Systems (FTS) in support of NASA's research, test, and development activities. You will be responsible for developing, evaluating, and/or establishing technical requirements for the application and integration of low voltage, electro-mechanical systems into aerospace vehicles (primarily on unmanned aircraft systems).

title: SUPERVISORY IT SPECIALIST (PLCYPLN)

It has been identified as a position necessary to carry out and support the mission of the US Cyber Command. It is in the Supervision Work Category at the Expert Work Level within the CES Occupational Structure. It is located in the DISA EMERGING TECHNOLOGY DIRECTORATE - REQS AND PROJECT ENGINEERING SUPPORT DIVISION.

title: Security Specialist (Direct Hire)
description: As a Physical Security Specialist performing the duties of the Center's Physical Security Program Manager, Facility Security Manager (FSM), Anti-Terrorism Officer (ATO), Critical Infrastructure Assurance Officer (CIAO), and assisting the Chief, Protective Services in the overall management and administration of the AFRC Protective Services Program, you will be responsible for establishing, revising, or reviewing policies, procedures, and meeting mission objectives.

title: Physical Scientist, AST, Science Program Management (Direct Hire)
description: NASA's Science Mission Directorate (SMD) engages the nation's science community, sponsors scientific research, and develops and deploys satellites and probes in collaboration with NASA's partners around the world to answer fundamental questions requiring the view from and into space. The Biological and Physical Sciences Division within SMD is seeking a qualified individual to serve as the Fundamental Physics Program Scientist.

description: As the Chief of Protective Services (CPS), you will be responsible for providing supervision, direction and oversight of the administration, planning, design, organization, services, operation, review, and analysis of security and law enforcement, emergency management, and fire services.

title: Engineering Technician (Direct Hire)
description: You will serve as Engineering Technician for the Engineering Division within the Engineering and Test Directorate. You will use your 3D modeling skills to generate 3D models as well as perform the checking function on 3D and 2D drawings to ensure modeling and drafting standards are met. You will use your knowledge of Creo, AutoCAD, Windchill, and specialized elements within those tools to define best practices. You will work with NASA Centers on Agency initiatives for Model Based Engineering.

organization: George C. Marshall Space Flight Center
description: You will serve as a security specialist responsible for the planning, integration, and oversight of a comprehensive Center-level security program supporting critical spaceflight, research, and engineering missions at NASA's Marshall Space Flight Center (MSFC).

title: Digital Modernization & Data Volunteer
locations: Anywhere in the U.S. (remote job), United States

The Semester of Service Program offers students a volunteer project-based opportunity to support Federal missions, gaining hands-on experience & valuable career-ready skills. Students must be enrolled at least half-time in an accredited trade school, technical or vocational institute, junior college, college, university, or other accredited educational institution, & participation must be with the permission of the institution at which the student is enrolled.

Openings: General Law; Policy and Rulemaking; Litigation, Employment and Labor Relations.

title: Planetary Defense Officer
description: NASA's Planetary Science Division, within the Science Mission Directorate, leads robotic exploration across the solar system-excluding the Sun and Earth-to advance understanding of planetary formation, evolution, and the potential for life. The Division also includes NASA's Planetary Defense Coordination Office, which oversees efforts to detect, track, and characterize asteroids and comets that may pose a threat to Earth.

organization: Office of the Assistant Secretary for Administration and Management
description: The Office of the Chief Information Officer (OCIO) serves as the IT hub of the U.S. Department of Labor. We develop, maintain and protect IT solutions and data across our 27 agencies to enable mission outcomes through technology and service. OCIO continually enhances federal IT and digital capability with a focus on cybersecurity and customer experience to serve America's wage earners, job seekers and retirees.

You will maintain oversight of the Center's environmental and occupational health programs and support administration of the occupational medical clinic.

title: Staff Assistant

The incumbent uses discretion and tact while communicating frequently within DEA and interagency contacts, typically at the highest levels of senior management.

title: Supervisory Intelligence Research Specialist

As a Supervisory Intelligence Research Specialist, you will oversee intelligence-related contingency planning, continuity of government activities, secure communications, and emergency operations for a 24/7 Intelligence Operations Center (IC), ensuring secure access to national security information for DOE stakeholders.

PC is comprised of the Strategic Capabilities Office (SCO) and Policy Strategy and Accountability (PSA) Office.

title: Chief Laboratories Development and Testing Division
description: The Chief, Laboratories, Development, and Testing Division is in the Engineering Directorate at NASA's Kennedy Space Center (KSC) and provides a discipline-based, technical expertise that offers a set of integrated capabilities in terms of the workforce, laboratories, and test facilities that deliver products and services across program and project life cycles at KSC, other NASA Centers, government agencies, and commercial partners.

title: Direct Hire Public Notice - Data Scientist
description: The U.S. Nuclear Regulatory Commission (NRC) will use Direct Hire Authority to fill vacancies for the Data Scientist positions within the agency. This vacancy is a REPOSITORY of applications. Applicants MAY BE periodically referred to Selecting Officials both during the open period and for up to 90 days after the closing date of the vacancy. Because of the large number of applications anticipated, applicants status will not be updated UNLESS referred.

title: Trial Attorney (Enforcement and Affirmative Litigation)
description: The Enforcement and Affirmative Litigation Branch seeks Trial Attorneys to oversee high-impact affirmative litigation that ensures nationwide compliance with federal law. The Branch offers a frontline role in the Department's major civil enforcement initiatives. Well-qualified candidates may be eligible for a signing bonus of up to $25,000, with priority given to applicants in D.C., Raleigh, San Francisco, Dallas, and N.Y.C. However, strong applicants from any location are encouraged to apply.

title: Trial Attorney (Enforcement and Affirmative Litigation)
description: The Enforcement and Affirmative Litigation Branch seeks Trial Attorneys to oversee high-impact affirmative litigation that ensures nationwide compliance with federal law. The Branch offers a frontline role in the Department's major civil enforcement initiatives. Well-qualified candidates may be eligible for a signing bonus of up to $25,000 depending on qualifications, education, and other factors. All recipients of a signing bonus are required to fulfill a service obligation.

title: Director, Center for Infrastructure Systems and Technology
organization: Office of the Secretary of Transportation
description: This position leads and oversees the Center for Infrastructure Systems and Technology within the Department of Transportation's Office of the Assistant Secretary for Research and Technology (OST-R), John A. Volpe National Transportation Center (Volpe), located in Cambridge, MA. The position is comprised of seven technical divisions with a combined federal staffing of more than 103 employees, a portfolio of 166 projects, and annual obligations of over $81million, on average.

title: Attorney Advisor (General)
description: This position is located in the Drug Enforcement Administrator (DEA), Office of Chief Counsel. This position primary purpose is to provide legal advice, review disciplinary proposals, defend DEA in employment-related administrative litigation, and support the defense of DEA and its employees in federal lawsuits. Department of Justice (DOJ) agencies post experienced attorney job announcements on the DOJ Careers page.

title: Foreign Service Office Management Specialist (FS 9017)
locations: Port of Spain, Trinidad and Tobago
District of Columbia, District of Columbia
description: The U.S. Department of State is developing a rank-ordered list of eligible hires for a limited number of Foreign Service Office Management Specialist (OMS) positions. Learn more about Foreign Service careers at https://careers.state.gov. There is more to joining the Foreign Service than just the base salary. Refer to the Benefits section for more information on total compensation.

title: IT Specialist
description: As an Information Technology (IT) Specialist in the Federal government, you will perform duties related to the installation and configuration of computer hardware, software, operating systems, networks, applications, and printers and scanners; providing technical support and troubleshooting issues; assisting with the maintenance of computer systems and networks; and ensuring the application of appropriate IT security measures.

title: PEGA Developer- DHA
description: IT Specialist (APPSW), PEGA Developer position(s) are being filled through the Office of Personnel Management's delegated Direct Hire Authority, open to all U.S. citizens. Selections made under this bulletin will be processed as new appointments to the civil service. Current civil service employees would, therefore, be given new appointments to the civil service. Under the provisions of the Direct Hire Authority, Veterans Preference and the "Rule of Many" do not apply.

title: Product Manager / Senior Product Manager
description: IT Specialist, Project Manager and Senior Project Manager position(s) are being filled through the Office of Personnel Management's delegated Direct Hire Authority, open to all U.S. citizens. Selections made under this bulletin will be processed as new appointments to the civil service. Current civil service employees would, therefore, be given new appointments to the civil service. Under the provisions of the Direct Hire Authority, Veterans Preference and the "Rule of Many" do not apply.
